돈나무 누나:
일론은 “내 회사들 사이에 예상치 못한 융합이 보인다”고 했어요. AI 세계에서 이기려면 무엇이 필요할까요? 바로 독점적 데이터예요.

Tesla의 자율주행 데이터, Neuralink의 멀티오믹스 데이터, 
우주 데이터, 다른 누구도 이런 데이터를 갖고 있지 않아요.

이렇한 융합은 2030년까지 100조 달러 규모 회사의 등장 가능성이 있게 합니다. 가장 유력한 후보는 제가 방금 말씀 드린 이유 때문에 "테슬라" 입니다.

kept.text: Uh I’m sorry what? https://t.co/p5StjkY3GA
CJ is Neuralink's 24th patient:

profile_bio.description: A YouTube channel all about Neuralink, hosted by @RyanTanaka.

quoted_tweet.text:
Thank you to everyone for your concern, prayers, and well wishes. We are safely home, and I will update you soon with some videos of my N1 at work. For now, I thought I would show you what the BCI wound line looks like when it heals. I was a little worried when Dr. Jagid first met me, looked at my head, and said, "Oh good, he doesn't have a lot of hair" so they wouldn't have to do a lot of work in shaving it. I wonder if HairClub for Men excludes brain implant patients.

The other photo is one of the two battery packs that Neuralink provides to all neuralnauts. The silver tray at the bottom holds the charger and, when plugged into a wall outlet using the Neuralink brick at the top, charges the charger. The black puck in the middle is the battery charger that sits in the silver tray. In order to charge my BCI, I unplug that cord and plug in the black puck to the silver tray using the cord you see attached to the puck. Then I hold the black puck near my BCI, turn the power on the silver tray, and the charger searches for, and connects with, my BCI. 

This is required for charging my implant and also for the initial connection to my laptop each time I use it. Neuralink gave me a beanie cap and two hats with holders sewn inside for the charger, positioned right over where my implant was specifically placed. They also provide an arm that holds the charger near your head while in bed, and a stand that holds it while you're in your wheelchair. Rereading this post makes me realize my explanation was as clear as mud. I hope you can make something of it.

quoted_tweet.text:
Neuralink President DJ Seo discusses the beginning days of the company in this short video clip.

The company was founded in 2016 by Elon Musk and eight other scientists and engineers.

Neuralink's mission is to:
"Create a generalized brain interface to restore autonomy to those with unmet medical needs today and unlock human potential tomorrow."

20+ human trial participants around the world have received these brain implants. The team will begin trials for their Blindsight product later this year to help the blind see again.

kept.text:
क्या एलन मस्क दिमाग में चिप लगा रहे हैं? हां, पर फिल्मों वाला सीन नहीं है।

1. Neuralink क्या है?

Elon Musk की कंपनी Neuralink एक छोटी सी चिप बनाती है जो सिक्के से भी छोटी है।

 इसे ऑपरेशन करके दिमाग में फिट किया जाता है। ये चिप दिमाग के सिग्नल पढ़ती है।

2. अभी ये किस काम आ रही है।
 
अभी 9 लोगों में ये चिप लग चुकी है। ये लोग गर्दन से नीचे पैरालाइज्ड हैं।  

पहला पेशेंट Noland Arbaugh अब सिर्फ सोचकर" लैपटॉप चलाता है, ऑनलाइन चेस खेलता है, मैसेज टाइप करता है। उसके हाथ काम नहीं करते, पर दिमाग से कर्सर चल जाता है।

3. अंधों के लिए भी चिप बन रही है?
  
हां। इसका नाम "Blindsight है। 2025-2026 में पहला इंसानी ट्रायल शुरू होगा। 
 
शुरुआत में इससे Atari गेम जैसी धुंधली सी रोशनी दिखेगी। Elon चाचा का दावा है कि बाद में ये नॉर्मल आंख से भी तेज हो सकती है।

4. सबसे जरूरी बात - ये चिप क्या नहीं करती।

ये आपकी सोच नहीं पढ़ सकती।  
ये आपके दिमाग को कंट्रोल नहीं कर सकती।  
ये लकवा या अंधापन जड़ से ठीक नहीं करती। 
 
ये सिर्फ दिमाग और कंप्यूटर के बीच "कीबोर्ड-माउस का काम करती है।

ये चिप बीमार लोगों को कंप्यूटर चलाने की आजादी दे रही है। दिमाग हैक करने वाली चीज नहीं है।
  
साइंस धीरे-धीरे चलता है, फिल्मों की तरह फटाफट नहीं।

kept.text:
머스크가 법정에서 꺼낸 말, “미국 자선단체 전체가 위험해질 수 있다”

- 일론 머스크가 OpenAI 재판에서 약 1시간 40분간 증언을 마쳤고 내일 증언이 이어질 예정임

- OpenAI는 원래 구글에 대응하는 비영리, 오픈소스, AI 안전 중심 조직으로 설립됐음

- 머스크의 AI에 대한 우려는 구글 공동창업자 래리 페이지와의 대화에서 시작됐는데, 페이지가 AI 위험을 진지하게 받아들이지 않는다고 느꼈음

- 오바마에게도 AI 위험성을 경고했지만 당시엔 AI가 위협적으로 느껴질 만큼 똑똑하지 않다는 반응을 받았고, 머스크는 “지금은 2026년이고 AI는 매우 똑똑하다”고 말함

- AI가 내년 안에 인간 지능을 넘어설 수 있으며, 신뢰할 수 없는 사람의 손에 들어가면 세계 전체에 위협이 된다고 봄

- SpaceX, Neuralink, xAI 모두 인류의 미래를 지키기 위한 큰 그림의 일환이라고 설명함

- OpenAI 설립 초기에는 비영리 법인이 중심이 되고 소규모 영리 법인이 자금을 지원하는 구조를 구상했음

- 샘 알트만과도 OpenAI가 501c3 자선단체여야 한다는 점에 합의했다고 증언함

- 영리 조직이 존재하는 건 괜찮지만 “꼬리가 몸통을 흔드는” 구조는 안 된다고 선을 그었음

- 구글의 핵심 인재였던 일리야 수츠케버를 OpenAI로 영입한 뒤 래리 페이지가 머스크와 연락을 끊었음

- 머스크의 핵심 주장은 OpenAI가 설립 원칙을 저버리고 영리 구조로 전환한 것 자체가 자선단체를 약탈하는 행위라는 것임

- “피고들이 무죄 판결을 받으면 이 판례가 미국 전체 자선단체를 약탈하는 면허가 될 것”이라고 강하게 경고함

quoted_tweet.text:
Here's a rough summary of Elon Musk’s 1 hour and 40 minute long testimony today during the OpenAI trial. He will resume his testimony tomorrow.

• Argues the case has huge implications: "It is not ok to steal a charity. If the defendants are found not guilty, this case will become caselaw. It’ll give license to looting every charity in America. The consequences of this case go far beyond me or everyone here. The entire foundation of charitable giving in America will be destroyed."

• Says OpenAI was founded as a nonprofit, open-source counterweight to Google, focused on AI safety.

• Claims the shift to a for-profit structure violated that mission.

• Says his AI concerns date back to conversations with Larry Page, who he felt wasn’t taking AI risk seriously.

• Elon says he tried to warn Obama about AI, but that Obama felt AI was not good enough (back then) to seem scary smart. "Here we are in 2026, AI is very smart."

• Believes AI could surpass human intelligence as soon as next year and poses existential risk in the hands of the wrong people: “If you have someone who’s not very trustworthy in charge of AI, that’s very dangerous for the whole world.”

• Elon framed his companies (SpaceX, Neuralink, xAI) as part of a broader mission to protect humanity’s future.

• Emphasized OpenAI’s original goal: AI for the good of humanity, not profit-driven control.

• Elon's main argument is that OpenAI abandoned its founding principles, and that precedent could reshape both AI governance and charitable trust.

• Larry Page refused to speak to Elon Musk again after Elon recruited Ilya Sutskever to join OpenAI. Elon viewed Ilya as the “number one” most valuable member at Google.

• Elon thought in the early days, OpenAI's corporate structure would be a nonprofit funded initially with donations, but there could potentially be a parallel for-profit that is owned by the nonprofit and funds the nonprofit: “We (Sam Altman and Elon) were in agreement that OpenAI would be a 501c3 charity.  

• Elon was not opposed to there being a small for-profit that provided funding to the nonprofit, as long as "the tail didn’t wag the dog."

• Elon: “there are very few people who understand venture capital in Silicon Valley like I do."
